primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend receivable can be designated as a hedged item in a documented hedge accounting relationship in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Section 6.3.5 establishes the general rule that only items with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items; Section 6.3.6 provides the exception permitting intragroup monetary items' foreign currency risk to qualify as a hedged item when it affects consolidated profit or loss
Defines monetary items, which is relevant for determining whether intragroup dividend receivables qualify for the Section 6.3.6 exception

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies the scope and application of hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on net investments and intragroup items, including conditions for qualifying hedged risk

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses disclosure of interests in subsidiaries and restrictions, not the recognition or eligibility criteria for hedge accounting
Deals with related party disclosure requirements, not hedge accounting treatment for intragroup items
Addresses dividend payable recognition and measurement, not hedging of dividend receivables

treatment_families.family: General exclusion of intragroup items from hedge accounting
Exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items affecting consolidated profit or loss
Hedging of net investment in foreign operation including intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: General intragroup exclusion from hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9.6.3.5, hedge accounting applies only to items with parties external to the reporting entity, excluding intragroup transactions and balances from being designated hedged items

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item foreign currency risk hedging
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9.6.3.6, foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when it results in exposure not fully eliminated on consolidation and affects consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Net investment hedge including intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 clarifies that hedging foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations may be applied to intragroup monetary items with external exposure characteristics
